teaching_modules.title: EEG frequencies and typical waveforms
learning_goals: Identify canonical EEG bands and relate waveform shape to frequency class.
Use waveform morphology as the base layer for downstream qEEG interpretation.
Anchor delta, theta, alpha, beta, and mu terminology to actual trace appearance.
topic_outline: Heather Ingram, Psy D, BCB, BCN
Manuel D. Halter, Ph.D., J.D., M.A.,
◦Raw EEG data is unfiltered brain wave
◦The raw EEG data collected from electrodes
is compressed over long periods of time to
obtain a tracing called an amplitude-integrated

clinical_use.analyzer: Use for band-level interpretation guardrails before describing spectral findings in clinical language.
clinical_use.raw_workbench: Use when counting oscillations manually, distinguishing band classes, and aligning raw waveform shape with qEEG outputs.

teaching_modules.title: Psychopharmacology part 1: clinical presentation confounds
learning_goals: Account for prescribed and non-prescribed substances before interpreting EEG-driven clinical presentation.
Identify caffeine and nicotine as practical qEEG confounds that alter arousal and training readiness.
topic_outline: Heather Ingram, Psy D, BCB, BCN
POTENTIAL EFFECTS OF PRESCRIBED AND NON-PRESCRIBED DRUGS ON CLINICAL PRESENTATION

clinical_use.analyzer: Use before interpreting arousal or symptom-linked qEEG findings when caffeine, nicotine, or medication exposure may be present.
clinical_use.raw_workbench: Use as a prescan checklist so activated or dysregulated raw EEG is not misread without substance context.

teaching_modules.title: Psychopharmacology part 2: EEG measure confounds
learning_goals: Map stimulants and illicit substances to likely alpha, theta, and beta changes on EEG.
Treat drug-related band shifts as confounds before assigning pathology labels.
topic_outline: Heather Ingram, Psy D, BCB, BCN
POTENTIAL EFFECTS OF PRESCRIBED AND NON-PRESCRIBED DRUGS ON EEG MEASURES
◦Amphetamines decrease alpha & theta activity and increase
◦People with high beta type ADHD often suffer from

clinical_use.analyzer: Use when a profile shows beta excess, alpha suppression, or reduced theta that may be medication-driven.
clinical_use.raw_workbench: Use when generalized fast activity or altered posterior alpha may reflect stimulant exposure rather than intrinsic dysfunction.

teaching_modules.title: Identifying and reducing artifacts
learning_goals: Recognize common recording artifacts before editing or reporting qEEG abnormalities.
Reduce false positives during manual raw review.
topic_outline: Manuel D. Halter, PhD, JD, MA,
◦Artifacts in EEG are any non-brain related electrical activity
or interfering electrical potentials that can be mistaken for
the actual EEG activity (Schwartz & Andrasik, 2003, p. 93

clinical_use.analyzer: Use as a guardrail before treating outlier power or asymmetry as brain-based.
clinical_use.raw_workbench: Use directly during channel-by-channel artifact review and segment rejection.

teaching_modules.title: Topographic maps and qEEG report writing
learning_goals: Interpret qEEG head maps without separating them from raw EEG and clinical context.
Translate findings into report language appropriate for clinicians and treatment planning.
topic_outline: Manuel D. Halter, PhD, JD, MA,
Heather Ingram, Psy D, BCB, BCN
HOW TO READ A TOPOGRAPHIC MAP (HEAD MAP)

clinical_use.analyzer: Use when summarizing map-based deviations into clinician-facing interpretations.
clinical_use.raw_workbench: Use after raw validation so report statements stay grounded in reviewed signal quality.

teaching_modules.title: Ethics, professional conduct, and efficacy criteria
learning_goals: Apply ethical and professional limits to qEEG communication and treatment claims.
Separate evidence-supported use cases from overreach.
topic_outline: QEEG Ethics and Professional Conduct Part 1
the dignity and worth of the

clinical_use.analyzer: Use when framing uncertainty, limitations, and strength of inference in AI-facing qEEG narratives.
clinical_use.raw_workbench: Use as a reporting boundary so technical observations are not overstated clinically.

teaching_modules.title: Abnormal EEG patterns
learning_goals: Recognize non-benign EEG patterns that require escalation beyond routine qEEG coaching.
Distinguish signal quality issues from clinically meaningful abnormal morphology.
topic_outline: Heather Ingram, Psy D, BCB, BCN
Manuel D. Halter, Ph.D., J.D., M.A.,

clinical_use.analyzer: Use as a caution layer before benignly summarizing patterns that may warrant formal EEG review.
clinical_use.raw_workbench: Use during manual review when spikes, slowing, or unusual morphology need escalation.

integration_notes: This bundle is intended for DeepSynaps qEEG analyzer and raw-workbench grounding, not as a replacement for clinical judgment.
Local qEEG courseware is listed ahead of external learning links so the product can rely on repo-native knowledge first.
Psychopharmacology content now comes from the actual local lesson PDFs rather than only certificate metadata.
